Desired:

**What You Will Actually Be Doing:** 
Engineer with the Latest Stack: Design and build AI-powered applications using modern AI orchestration frameworks such as LangChain and LlamaIndex. Deliver the best tech stack, ensuring the team stays at the forefront of applied AI innovation
Be a Workflow Disruptor: Leverage your hands-on experience with low-code automation platforms to rapidly prototype and deploy AI connectors that automate critical business processes.
Proactively apply advanced prompt engineering techniques to iteratively optimize large language model (LLM) performance, ensuring output consistency, mitigating biases, and maximizing utility across diverse and complex use cases.
Drive Reliability: Apply engineering rigor—automated tests (unit, integration, data validation), version control with reproducible builds, CI/CD, and end-to-end observability—so models graduate from experimental notebooks to trusted, production-grade services.
Demonstrate Value: Showcase a track record of process automation—demonstrating successfully applied AI delivering tangible operational efficiency gains.

**Your success factors**
3+ Years in a role focused on productionizing AI/ML solutions (e.g., AI/ML Engineer, Applied Data Scientist).
Deep practical experience with LangChain, LlamaIndex, LangGraph, or similar AI frameworks.
Proven ability to design and implement robust Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems—combining vector databases (e.g., Pinecone, Weaviate) with document loaders, embeddings, and retrieval evaluation techniques.
Strong Python expertise with production experience in frameworks such as FastAPI, Flask, and deployment of containerized services (Docker, Kubernetes).
Solid MLOps foundation and treating AI pipelines as scalable software.
Experience with cloud environments (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and associated data services.
A proven ability to leverage low-code tools like n8n, Langflow to accelerate deployment and integration.
Proven ability to mentor engineers, make architectural trade-offs, and drive AI product strategy through strong technical and business alignment.

**Your benefits**
We Are a Great Place to Work
Our tolerant and empathic work environment enables you to help shape our company’s future, thereby giving you lots of room for personal development. Our unique company culture was officially recognised by the Great Place to Work institute.
We Improve Your Work-Life Balance
Flexitime and hybrid working arrangements as well as a special leave allowance enable you to organise your workday around your needs. And we care about your children. Our head office in Hirschau operates a child day-care centre that looks after your little ones.
We Communicate at a Peer Level
We are a family-run business. Means friendly interaction is paramount to us. And when it comes to being a trust-based horizontal organisation, around here, we practise what we preach.
We Promote Health
Which is why we offer a variety of services and activities centred around health and fitness. Regular medical checkups are top of the agenda. Moreover, for all those who like to keep fit, there is Conrad Sports Club based at our Hirschau office that provides a range of activities, coaching sessions and even an in-house tennis court. Being healthy isn’t just about exercising, it’s also about a balanced diet. This is why food served in our cafeterias at Hirschau and Wernberg uses local produce and is freshly prepared.

German language is a plus, Fluent English is necessary

language proficiency:

Education & Experience
MSc or PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Data Science, or related field.
5+ years of experience in applied ML/AI, with strong expertise in Generative AI and NLP.
Demonstrated success in designing and deploying production-ready LLM-powered systems.

**Key Skills And Experience**

You have a Master’s degree in computer science or a comparable education (e.g. mathematics). A PhD in a machine learning related field is a plus.

German language skills are required. Very good English skills complete your profile.

ML & GenAI Erfahrung: 3–5 Jahre Berufserfahrung in der Entwicklung und Operationalisierung von Machine-Learning- oder GenAI-Lösungen
GenAI-Frameworks gemeistert: Fundierte Kenntnisse in der Umsetzung von GenAI-Anwendungen, z. B. mit Large Language Models (LLMs), Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) oder Frameworks wie LangChain / LangGraph
Prompt & API Expertise: Erfahrung im Prompt Engineering, in der Integration von KI-APIs (z. B. OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure OpenAI) sowie im Aufbau von LLM-basierten Pipelines

Teamgeist & Innovationsfreude: Teamgeist, Eigeninitiative und Begeisterung, neue KI-Technologien in die Praxis zu bringen
Kommunikationstalent: Du bringst sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse sowie eine Bereitschaft zur deutschlandweiten projektbedingten Mobilität mit

Must-Have:
- Strong background in machine learning, deep learning, or reinforcement learning.

- Experience with end-to-end ML systems, from preprocessing and feature extraction to training, evaluation, and deployment.
- Comfort with MLOps tools (e.g., Weights & Biases, MLflow, Docker, Kubernetes, or Airflow).

Abgeschlossenes Studium und mehrjährige relevante Berufserfahrung mit der Mircosoft (Fabric) Plattform in Rollen wie Data Engineer, Developer, Analyst, Data Scientist, ML-Engineer oder Administrator

Expertise in Microsoft Fabric sowie der Integration mit Azure-Services und Power Plattform und Erfahrungen mit SQL, Python, Datenmodellierungen und Power BI Architekturen

meta.title: Senior Software Engineer / Algorithmenentwickler (Tracking & Fusion, AI/ML-Integration)

data.text:
What You’ll Do
As a Machine Learning Engineer III on the Routing Cost team, you will lead the design, development, and delivery of highly trafficked recommendation systems for our routing cost engine. Your technical contributions will include:

Lead the engineering team(s) to execute end-to-end feature implementation within the larger navigation organization (~80 engineers).
Design and build new ML and data pipelines, as well as optimizing existing ML and data pipelines.
Build tools for automated quality assurance and data exploration.
Leverage geospatial algorithms to optimize pipelines, as well as creating automated tuning and outlier detection algorithms.
Partner with data scientists to make improvements to our models for determining patterns in movement and/or traffic.
Mentor colleagues and participate in code and design reviews.
Participate in an on-call rotation to ensure our systems remain available to customers 24/7. Team members alternate as the on-call primary responder, which may require immediate response outside normal working hours, including weekends.

What We Believe Are Important Traits For This Role
8-10+ years of backend and ML experience: Extensive hands-on experience showcasing expertise and a strong track record in the engineering field.
Technical knowledge: Extensive experience with Python, distributed processing pipelines (Hadoop, Spark, Airflow, Dask), and very large datasets – statistical analysis, data quality control, optimizing storage.
Cloud proficiency: Extensive experience with cloud and infrastructure services, including, as well as deploying and managing infrastructure in cloud environments.
Operational excellence: You drive a culture of quality and continuous improvement in your organization. Strong passion for clean code and robust architecture, with a drive to elevate engineering practices and design standards across the team.
Ownership & execution: Own the technical vision, lead the strategy and guide the team to execute towards building the future of our routing cost engine.
Customer focus: You care deeply about your customers and their problems, and you work tirelessly to innovate on their behalf.
Growth mindset: Have the humility to accept mistakes, learn and grow from them.
Mentorship: Experience mentoring and leading engineering teams developing skill sets and career growth.
Strong written and vocal communication skills: You can talk and write about complex ideas concisely to a wide audience. You are able to lead and resolve org-wide technical discussions and are able to collaborate and include input from cross-functional teams (security, devops, data, compliance and product).
Data informed mindset: You believe in the value of data informed decision making and strive to measure things that are not straight forward but essential. You also have the right instincts to make speedy decisions in the absence of perfect & comprehensive data.

Minimum Qualifications:

Experience with financial analysis, fundraising support, or investor materials is a strong plus.
Strong interest and prior experience in defence, security, aerospace, robotics, or dual-use technologies.
Ability to navigate sensitive or confidential information with absolute professionalism.
Highly structured working style, strong analytical and problem-solving skills, and an ability to “figure things out” without perfect guidance.
Comfortable working in an early-stage environment with shifting priorities and hands-on tasks.
Excellent communication skills internally and externally.

Essential Skills
Deep ML experience in video modelling, generative models, sequence modelling, world models, RL, or simulation-based learning.
Strong software engineering ability: writing clean, modular Python and building ML systems end-to-end; including implementing architectures, training loops, and data pipelines from scratch.
Real-world ML mindset: experience with noisy, multimodal, temporal, or large-scale datasets (not just clean academic examples).
Engineering maturity: ability to debug, profile, optimize, and scale ML systems beyond prototype level.
PyTorch expertise: hands-on experience with training pipelines, distributed workloads, or deployment workflows.

Beneficial Skills
Multimodal intuition: combining signals such as vision, pose, audio, force/tactile, or simulation state.
Systems sense: familiarity with streaming architectures, real-time constraints, microservices, or serialization formats (e.g., Protobuf / MCAP).
Embodied-AI curiosity: interest in teleoperation interfaces, robot interaction, or real-world control dynamics.
Simulation experience: knowledge of Isaac Sim, MuJoCo, Unity, Unreal, or related simulation platforms.

Das bringst Du mit:
Abgeschlossenes Studium der Fachrichtung Informatik oder eine abgeschlossene IT-technische Ausbildung in Verbindung mit einer Weiterbildung zum staatlich geprüften Techniker (w/m/d) inkl. mehrjähriger Berufserfahrung in der Programmierung oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation
Fundierte Kenntnisse mit SQL/Oracle Datenbanken sowie in den Programmiersprachen Python/Java und ähnliche
Gute Kenntnisse der Microsoft-Produkte sowie in Prozessautomatisierung
Sehr gute Kenntnisse in Netzwerkkommunikationsprotokollen sowie ein ausgeprägtes Verständnis für Unternehmensprozesse
Umfangreiche Erfahrungen im IT-Projekt-/Stakeholder-Management
Hohe Einsatzbereitschaft und Organisationsfähigkeiten sowie ausgeprägte Team- und Kommunikationsfähigkeit

Qualifikationen
PhD (with 4+ years relevant experience) in life sciences (biology, pharmacology, chemistry, biotechnology, bioinformatics, pharmacy, medicine, biomedical data science, or related). Equivalent experience considered at Master's (10+ years) or Bachelor's (12+ years) level. Pharmaceutical industry experience strongly preferred.
Deep knowledge of emerging therapeutic modalities, such as biotherapeutics (ADCs, mAbs, multispecific antibodies, cell therapies, etc.), different types of degraders, gene therapies (AAV, lentiviral, CAR-T, CRISPR, etc.), RNA therapies (mRNA, siRNA, ASO), as shown in recent industry advances.
Hands-on expertise with extraction and integration of knowledge from literature, patents, competitive intelligence databases (e.g., Citeline, Cortellis), and news/press release sources.
Competence in using GenAI models for knowledge extraction. Solid knowledge of applications and pitfalls in applying current GenAI models, their prompting, and embedding them in larger Python-based workflows to automate knowledge extraction tasks. Basic understanding of agents and agentic workflows (MCP, A2A).
Solid background in programming, informatics, and data science. Proficiency in Python required; familiarity with data engineering, software development principles, relational databases and SQL as well as common formats/APIs. Knowledge of data pipeline frameworks, text mining, NLP, semantic enrichment, ontologies is a plus.
Ability to rapidly learn and adapt to new advances in therapeutic modalities and information technologies, such as generative AI and agents, reflecting the dynamic and expanding landscape.
Demonstrated leadership in designing and delivering creative technology solutions that advance business and scientific objectives; proven ability to translate complex scientific/business questions into actionable, large-scale information solutions, also to non-scientific audiences and different leadership levels.
Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills, with superior attention to detail and high degree of reliability and integrity. Comfortable working in fast-paced, dynamic, multidisciplinary, and collaborative settings, and balancing multiple projects with constantly shifting priorities.
Demonstrates scientific curiosity, technical creativity, and a proactive drive for innovation to break new ground in the field of information analysis and insights generation.
Excellent written and oral English language skills as well as the openness to travel (10%)
